title: Signal Code Registry
description: Canonical append-only registry of tournament-eligible signal codes (per ECON-DS3). Single source of truth for the `signal_code` field in `results/{pair_id}/winner_summary.json` (ECON-H5) and for the `signal` column in every tournament results CSV. Entries are stable across reruns — dropping a signal from a pipeline does NOT free its code for reuse. Adding a signal requires a PR + regression_note entry (per META-VNC) + sop-changelog entry. The registry is authored by Evan; all other agents read. Per META-CF (Contract File Standard), this schema ships with a companion instance (`docs/schemas/signal_code_registry.json`) and example (`docs/schemas/examples/signal_code_registry.example.json`).

x-version.description: Semver of the registry instance (separate from this schema's x-version). Bumped per META-CF whenever signals are added. Minor bumps are additive (append-only); major bumps require Lead approval and a sop-changelog entry.

x-owner.description: Single agent accountable for the registry instance. Must be 'evan'.

x-generated.description: ISO-8601 date the instance was last regenerated.

notes.description: Freeform top-level notes about the instance (e.g., what was added in this version, audit references).

signals.description: Ordered append-only list of registered signals. Insertion order is the canonical registration order; new entries append to the end. Never reorder or delete existing entries.

signal_entry.description: One registered signal. Identity is the `signal_code`; all other fields describe it.

signal_code.description: Stable canonical identifier. snake_case. NOT prefixed with `S<number>_` — the legacy registration-order prefix convention is deprecated. Example: `hmm_stress`, `hmm_3state`, `markov_regime`, `zscore_lb252`. This is the exact string written to `winner_summary.json.signal_code` and to the tournament CSV `signal` column.

display_name.description: Human-readable label used on portal cards and captions. Example: `HMM 2-state stress probability`.

parquet_column_pattern.description: Canonical column name (or regex) in `results/{pair_id}/signals_{date}.parquet` that carries the signal's time series. Example: `hmm_2state_prob_stress`. MUST match the column Ace's `pd.read_parquet(...)[signal_column]` call reads at render time.

description.description: One to two sentence description of what the signal measures and the economic interpretation.

source_method.description: The econometric method that produces the signal. Canonical vocabulary: `hmm_2state`, `hmm_3state`, `markov_switching`, `zscore`, `percentile_rank`, `roc`, `composite`, `transfer_entropy`, `ccf_residual`, `cusum`, `gmm_cluster`, `jenks_break`. New methods append to this list only with Lead approval.

added_at.description: ISO-8601 date the entry was first appended to the registry.

first_pair.description: The `pair_id` whose rerun first required this signal (audit-trail pointer; optional for bootstrap entries).

notes.description: Freeform — sign convention, regime-labeling convention, known pitfalls, deprecation markers.
